description: Phase 11 — Filters. Add region and difficulty filters to the beach listing via a bottom sheet opened from the header. Multi-select within each filter, AND across filters (region AND difficulty AND favourites), with selections persisted to IndexedDB.

userStories.title: Add region + difficulty filter state and persistence to beachStore
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the store to manage region/difficulty selections and persist them to IndexedDB so filters survive reloads.
acceptanceCriteria: beachStore state extended with selectedRegions: Set<string> and selectedDifficulties: Set<string> (both initialised empty)
New actions: toggleRegion(region: string), toggleDifficulty(level: string), clearFilters() — clearFilters clears regions, difficulties, AND showFavouritesOnly

toggleRegion / toggleDifficulty add or remove the value from the Set and persist to db.settings under keys 'selectedRegions' and 'selectedDifficulties' (stored as arrays via Array.from(set) since IndexedDB cannot serialise Set)
toggleFavouritesFilter updated to also persist the new value to db.settings under key 'showFavouritesOnly'

displayedBeaches getter updated: a beach passes when (selectedRegions is empty OR selectedRegions has beach.region) AND (selectedDifficulties is empty OR selectedDifficulties has beach.skillLevel) AND (not showFavouritesOnly OR favourites has beach.id). Empty Sets mean no filter applied — they do NOT exclude all beaches
New getter activeFilterCount: number — returns count of active filters (selectedRegions.size > 0 counts 1, selectedDifficulties.size > 0 counts 1, showFavouritesOnly true counts 1 → max 3)
New getter availableRegions: string[] — unique sorted list of beach.region across state.beaches
New getter availableDifficulties: string[] — unique sorted list of beach.skillLevel across state.beaches

New action hydrateSettings() reads 'selectedRegions', 'selectedDifficulties', and 'showFavouritesOnly' from db.settings and populates state; defensively defaults to empty Sets / false if a stored value is missing, null, or not an array/boolean
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)
userStories.notes: The db.settings table (Dexie, primary key 'key') is already declared in ui/src/db.ts — no schema migration. Filter logic lives entirely in the displayedBeaches getter; do not mutate state.beaches. Existing toggleFavouritesFilter already exists — extend it to persist, don't replace it.

userStories.title: Hydrate persisted filters on app startup
userStories.description: As a user, I want my previously selected filters to still be applied when I reopen the app.
acceptanceCriteria: The existing call site for beachStore.hydrateFavourites() is located (search ui/src for 'hydrateFavourites(') and beachStore.hydrateSettings() is invoked in the same place, before or alongside the favourites hydration
If no settings exist in IndexedDB yet, hydration silently succeeds with default empty filters (no thrown errors, no console errors)
Hydration failures are caught and logged to console.error; the app still renders and functions
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: open the app, toggle favourites-only (and later after US-003, toggle some region/difficulty filters), reload the page, confirm the filters are still applied
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (hydrateSettings must exist). The existing hydrateFavourites call is likely in App.vue or a setup in main.ts — search and mirror the pattern exactly.

userStories.title: Build FilterSheet.vue with region + difficulty multi-select checkboxes
userStories.description: As a user, I want a bottom sheet with clear checkboxes for each region and difficulty so I can pick multiple values.
acceptanceCriteria: New component file ui/src/components/FilterSheet.vue (Vue SFC, NOT a Lit component) that wraps <surf-bottom-sheet>
Component accepts prop `open: boolean` and emits `close` event when user taps the Done button or the sheet's built-in close/backdrop (consume the close event from surf-bottom-sheet)
Sheet has title 'Filters'

Two vertically stacked sections inside the sheet: 'Region' (list of checkbox rows, one per value in beachStore.availableRegions) and 'Difficulty' (list of checkbox rows, one per value in beachStore.availableDifficulties)
Each region row's checkbox is checked when beachStore.selectedRegions has that value; clicking the row or checkbox calls beachStore.toggleRegion(region)

Each difficulty row's checkbox is checked when beachStore.selectedDifficulties has that value; clicking the row calls beachStore.toggleDifficulty(level). Display labels are capitalised (e.g. 'beginner' → 'Beginner') while the stored value stays lowercased
Each row is a tappable area with a minimum 44px touch target
Footer has two buttons: 'Clear all' calls beachStore.clearFilters() but does NOT close the sheet; 'Done' emits the close event
When beachStore.availableRegions or availableDifficulties is empty, that section shows a short 'No options available' message
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: render the sheet in HomeView (open it via a temporary button or after US-004), toggle a region checkbox, confirm the visible beach list updates; tap Clear all, confirm both Sets empty
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Use native <input type='checkbox'> styled with scoped SCSS — do not create a new Lit component. The favourites-only toggle stays in the header; it is NOT inside this sheet.

userStories.title: Add filter button to HomeView header with active-count indicator
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clearly marked filter button in the header that shows when filters are active.
acceptanceCriteria: New <button> added to HomeView.vue header, placed BETWEEN the favourites toggle (.fav-filter-btn) and the install button (.install-btn)

Button renders <surf-icon name='sliders-horizontal'> (or 'filter' if sliders-horizontal is not available in the surf-icon registry — check ui/src/components/lit/surf-icon.ts and pick one; add it to the registry if missing by importing the matching lucide icon)
Button has aria-label='Open filters', minimum 44x44px touch target, and styling consistent with the adjacent header buttons (transparent background, var(--space-2) padding)
When beachStore.activeFilterCount > 0, a small coloured dot badge is rendered over the icon using an existing token (e.g. var(--color-surf-very-good) or a similar accent)
Local ref `filterSheetOpen = ref(false)` is added to HomeView.vue script setup
Clicking the button sets filterSheetOpen.value = true
<FilterSheet :open='filterSheetOpen' @close='filterSheetOpen = false' /> is rendered in HomeView.vue template, imported from the new component
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: click the filter button, sheet opens; select a region; tap Done; sheet closes; dot badge appears on the filter button; beach list is filtered to that region
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 (FilterSheet component). Do not restyle the existing favourites, install, or refresh buttons — only add the new filter button alongside them. The favourites-only toggle still counts toward activeFilterCount even though its control is outside the sheet.

userStories.title: Empty state when filters exclude every beach
userStories.description: As a user, when my filters match no beaches, I want a clear message and a one-tap way to clear filters.
acceptanceCriteria: In HomeView.vue, a new empty-state block renders when beachStore.beaches.length > 0 AND beachStore.displayedBeaches.length === 0 AND beachStore.activeFilterCount > 0 AND the existing favourites-only empty state is NOT already showing (i.e. region OR difficulty filter is contributing to the emptiness, not favourites-only alone)

The new empty state mirrors the existing favourites empty state visually: centred (same icon as the filter button), an

'No beaches match your filters', a

'Try removing a filter or clearing them all', and a labelled 'Clear filters' that calls beachStore.clearFilters()
The existing favourites-only empty state continues to fire when only the favourites filter is active and it alone produces zero results — no regression
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: activate filters that produce zero matches (e.g. pick a region and a difficulty combination no beach satisfies); the new empty state appears; clicking 'Clear filters' restores the full list
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (activeFilterCount, clearFilters) and US-004 (filter button uses same icon). Condition ordering matters — keep the favourites-only empty state check first so it still fires on its own; the new state fires when any OTHER filter contributes to zero results.
